settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage your organization — members, taxonomy, API access and governance.

separation.label: Separation of duties
separation.on: Authors cannot review their own items — a different reviewer must approve.
separation.off: Authors may review and approve their own items.

descriptions.admin: Manages members, taxonomy, API credentials and governance policy.
descriptions.curator: Creates and edits knowledge items and submits them for review.
descriptions.reviewer: Approves or rejects items in review; deprecates and archives them.
descriptions.auditor: Reads the full audit trail of every change.
descriptions.consumer: Searches and reads published knowledge through the API / MCP.

sensitivity.heading: Sensitivity levels
sensitivity.intro: Every item carries a sensitivity. API credentials set a ceiling that caps what they can read.
sensitivity.public: Readable by anyone the credential or session allows.
sensitivity.confidential: The most restricted tier — served the least widely.

users.title: Users & roles
users.disableDescription: Disabling {{email}} will revoke their access until re-enabled.

tags.title: Tenant tags
tags.removeDescription: "{{label}}" will be removed. Tags that are in use cannot be removed.

credentials.title: Consumer credentials
credentials.revokeDescription: Applications using it will stop working.

secret.title: Credential secret
secret.warning: Copy this secret now. It is shown only once and cannot be retrieved again.

mcp.heading: Connect your AI client (MCP)
mcp.subtitle: Paste the config below into your chosen client. We only list clients compatible with a remote, API-key-authenticated MCP server.

claude-code: Run this command in the terminal where Claude Code is installed.
Restart Claude Code; the domain-expert tools appear under /mcp.
claude-desktop: Open Claude Desktop → Settings → Developer → Edit Config.
Paste this into claude_desktop_config.json (merge with any existing mcpServers).
Save and fully restart Claude Desktop.
Requires Node.js (npx) — mcp-remote bridges the HTTP server.
cursor: Open (or create) ~/.cursor/mcp.json — or use Settings → MCP → Add new server.
Paste this (merge with any existing mcpServers) and save.
Reopen Cursor and enable domain-expert in Settings → MCP.
vscode: Create .vscode/mcp.json in your workspace (or run “MCP: Add Server” from the Command Palette).
Click “Start” on the server. Requires VS Code 1.102 or newer.
windsurf: Open Windsurf → Settings → Cascade → MCP Servers → “Add custom server” (or edit ~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json).
Paste this (merge with any existing mcpServers) and save.
Click refresh in the MCP panel.
generic: Any MCP client that accepts a remote (Streamable HTTP) server with a custom header works.
Point it at the URL below and send the Authorization: Bearer … header on every request.

policy.title: Org policy
policy.requireReviewerHint: The approver must differ from the author.
